---
title: "Week 4 — scRNA-seq II (annotation, capstone)"
subtitle: "Cell-type annotation, literature, protocols, and your scoped capstone"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Learning objectives
- Apply marker-based and reference-based cell-type annotation (CellTypist) and exercise the **Discernment** D against the AI's biological claims.
- Run a small differential-expression analysis between conditions; own the interpretation in your own words (**Diligence**).
- Use grounded literature tools to verify every citation in a short literature brief.
- Use AI as a sparring partner for protocol design without ceding scientific judgement.
- Deliver a 4-D's-aware capstone with a complete disclosure statement.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Scope reminder
The capstone was *scoped in week 3*. Week 4 is execution and writeup, not start-to-finish. An honest 4-week course cannot deliver an "end-to-end research project" cold.
:::

## Topics

- AI-assisted cell-type annotation (markers + CellTypist) and differential expression.
- AI-assisted literature review with verification.
- AI as a sparring partner for protocol design.
- Capstone execution and presentations.

## Before class

Read:

- [Literature review](../bioinformatics/literature-review.qmd) — verification workflow + worked fabrication catch.
- [Protocol design](../bioinformatics/protocol-design.qmd) — AI-sparring worked example with two AI-was-wrong moments.
- [Module 5 — Annotation & interpretation](../modules/module-05-annotation-interpretation/README.md) — full module reading; you will run it in class.

Setup:

- Capstone proposal (from week 3) finalised after instructor feedback.
- For students continuing the PBMC 3k thread: post-clustering AnnData ready (output of [Module 4](../modules/module-04-clustering-umap/README.md)). For students on a non-genomics capstone: relevant data, papers, or protocol drafts collected.

## In class

**Module 5 walkthrough (~40 min):** instructor leads marker-gene discovery (`sc.tl.rank_genes_groups`), dotplot inspection, CellTypist automated annotation, manual override of disagreements, differential expression between conditions, and export of the annotated UMAP. Discernment moves are narrated explicitly: *when to trust CellTypist*, *what the AI's interpretation gets right and wrong*, *organism-conventions traps* (e.g., `MS4A1` vs. `Ms4a1`).

**Demo: literature & protocol AI sparring (~30 min):** instructor triages a new subfield with a grounded literature tool (verifying every citation against PubMed/CrossRef), then iterates on a protocol with an AI sparring partner — generate, critique, refine.

**Capstone working session and peer feedback (~30 min):** focused work on the deliverable.

**Lightning presentations (~20 min):** 3-minute presentations per student. The 4 D's are the rubric; what did each look like in your work?

## After class

- **Capstone** *(30%)*: deliver on the scoped proposal from week 3. Pick **one** of three paths (chosen at week 3 scoping):
  - **Path A — scRNA-seq analysis on PBMC 3k.** Continue the mini-project: marker-based annotation, CellTypist, one differential-expression contrast, and an annotated UMAP figure. ([Module 5](../modules/module-05-annotation-interpretation/README.md) is the runnable reference.)
  - **Path B — literature brief.** A scoped review of a question relevant to your work, ≤ 5 verified citations each with a one-sentence relevance note. Verification means each citation has been checked on PubMed or CrossRef per the [verification workflow](../bioinformatics/literature-review.qmd).
  - **Path C — protocol design.** A one-page wet-lab or computational protocol, designed using AI as a sparring partner per [Protocol design](../bioinformatics/protocol-design.qmd). Include controls, sample-size reasoning, expected outcomes, and at least one place where you rejected an AI suggestion with a documented reason.

  Whichever path you pick, every capstone must include:

  1. The deliverable (analysis notebook, literature brief, or protocol).
  2. **4 D's reflection** (≤ 500 words): map your process onto Delegation, Description, Discernment, Diligence. Where did each show up? Where did you fail and recover?
  3. **Disclosure statement** per the [rubric](../course-syllabus.qmd#disclosure-rubric).

## Wrap-up

- What worked, what didn't.
- Commitments: what habits will you keep after the course?
- Honest acknowledgment: this is a *foundations* course. Real fluency develops over months of supervised practice. Plan accordingly.

## Supplementary

- [Module 5 — Annotation & interpretation](../modules/module-05-annotation-interpretation/README.md).
- [Literature review](../bioinformatics/literature-review.qmd) and [Protocol design](../bioinformatics/protocol-design.qmd) — capstone Path B and Path C reference.
- Slides, recording, and notebooks will be posted here after each session.
